skill.name: terraform-module-library
skill.description: Build reusable Terraform modules for AWS, Azure, and GCP infrastructure following infrastructure-as-code best practices. Use when creating infrastructure modules, standardizing cloud provisioning, or implementing reusable IaC components.
skill.summary: Build reusable Terraform modules for AWS, Azure, and GCP infrastructure.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown code fences, Terraform source paths, private CIDR examples, Go import syntax, and weak-crypto keywords. Manual review found these are documentation examples, not executable code, network access, command execution, filesystem traversal, or cryptographic implementation. No prompt injection or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: False positive: Markdown examples flagged as command execution
low_findings.description: The flagged locations are markdown code fences, inline file names, Terraform snippets, or related-skill references. They do not execute shell commands or pass user input to a command interpreter.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is fenced markdown or inline documentation, not executable Ruby or shell syntax. No command runner or script file is present.

low_findings.title: False positive: Go import block flagged as dynamic import
low_findings.description: The import statement appears inside a Terratest example in a markdown code block. It is static Go example text and is not a dynamic JavaScript import expression.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding code block is labeled as Go test example content. There is no JavaScript runtime or dynamic import invocation.

low_findings.title: False positive: Example CIDR blocks flagged as network activity
low_findings.description: The flagged IP-like values are private RFC1918 CIDR ranges used in a Terraform VPC example. They do not define outbound network requests or external endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The values are private subnet examples inside Terraform documentation. No HTTP client, socket use, or data transfer logic was found.

low_findings.title: False positive: Terraform relative paths flagged as traversal
low_findings.description: The relative paths are Terraform module source paths and a Terratest example directory. They are documentation examples, not file access logic that reads arbitrary paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths are inside example HCL and Go snippets. No skill code uses these paths to access the local filesystem.

low_findings.title: False positive: Weak cryptography keywords in prose
low_findings.description: The flagged weak-crypto matches occur in ordinary Terraform documentation text such as descriptions, identifiers, IDs, and rule descriptions. No cryptographic algorithm is implemented or recommended.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar cryptographic implementation. The matches are terms embedded in benign prose and infrastructure examples.

low_findings.title: False positive: Reconnaissance keywords in resource examples
low_findings.description: The flagged reconnaissance terms are VPC IDs, subnet IDs, DNS settings, and similar Terraform output names. They are not commands that enumerate the host system.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged terms are Terraform attribute names and output examples. No host reconnaissance command or environment enumeration was found.

content.value_statement: Teams often duplicate Terraform code across cloud projects and lose consistency. This skill provides reusable module patterns, examples, and review guidance for AWS, Azure, and GCP infrastructure.
actual_capabilities: Explains a standard Terraform module layout with main, variables, outputs, versions, examples, and tests.
Provides an AWS VPC module example with variables, outputs, tags, and optional internet gateway creation.
Shows module composition patterns for VPC and RDS dependencies.
Lists AWS module categories such as VPC, EKS, RDS, S3, ALB, Lambda, and security groups.
Recommends module practices such as semantic versioning, provider pinning, input validation, examples, and Terratest coverage.
limitations: Only the AWS reference file is included in this package, even though Azure and GCP are mentioned.
Referenced asset folders and some reference files are not present in the scanned file structure.
The skill provides examples and guidance but does not run Terraform, validate plans, or inspect cloud accounts.
Security and compliance requirements still need organization-specific review.

use_cases.title: Create a Shared Module Template
use_cases.description: Define the baseline folder structure, inputs, outputs, examples, and tests for reusable Terraform modules.

use_cases.title: Standardize AWS Infrastructure Patterns
use_cases.description: Use the included AWS module categories and VPC example to align teams on repeatable infrastructure design.

use_cases.title: Review Module Readiness
use_cases.description: Check whether a module has documentation, validation, version constraints, tagging, outputs, and Terratest coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Basic Module
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design a reusable Terraform module for an AWS VPC. Include files, required variables, outputs, and a complete example structure.

prompt_templates.title: Improve an Existing Module
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Terraform module design for reusable infrastructure. Focus on variables, outputs, tags, provider constraints, examples, and testing gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Compose Multiple Modules
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Terraform composition that connects VPC and RDS modules. Show the data flow between outputs and inputs, and explain dependency boundaries.

prompt_templates.title: Define Organization Standards
prompt_templates.prompt: Create Terraform module standards for a platform team. Include versioning, documentation, testing, validation, tagging, and release expectations.

output_examples.input: Create a reusable AWS VPC module plan.
output_examples.output: A module outline with required files, input variables for CIDR and availability zones, outputs for VPC and subnet IDs, tagging guidance, and a complete example directory.

output_examples.input: Review this module for production readiness.
output_examples.output: A checklist-style review covering provider pinning, input validation, outputs, examples, Terratest coverage, tags, documentation, and versioning.

output_examples.input: Plan module composition for VPC and database resources.
output_examples.output: A dependency map showing the VPC module outputs consumed by the database module, plus notes on naming, subnet selection, and environment tags.

best_practices: Document every input and output so downstream teams can use modules without reading implementation details.
Pin provider versions and use semantic versioning for module releases.
Add examples and Terratest coverage before promoting a module to shared use.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode environment-specific values inside reusable modules.
Do not publish modules without examples, validation, and output documentation.
Do not assume the included examples satisfy every security or compliance policy.

faq.question: Does this skill generate complete Terraform modules?
faq.answer: It provides structure, examples, and guidance that can be used to draft modules. Final code still needs review and testing.

faq.question: Which cloud providers does it cover?
faq.answer: The skill describes AWS, Azure, and GCP module organization. The included detailed reference file focuses on AWS patterns.

faq.question: Can it run Terraform commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation and guidance only. It does not execute Terraform, shell commands, or cloud API calls.

faq.question: Does it include testing guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends Terratest and shows a basic test pattern for module validation.

faq.question: Is this suitable for regulated environments?
faq.answer: It can help create consistent modules, but regulated environments need separate policy, access, and compliance review.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, or Claude Code use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code compatible workflows.
